title: England.
content:
Where'er I go in this dense East,
        In sunshine or shade,
     I retch at the villainous feast
        That England has made.

     And my shame cannot understand,
        As scorn springs elate,
     How I ever loved that land
        That now I hate!
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Francis William Lauderdale Adams delivers a powerful performance in "England."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
